rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JAMES WON LING BONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Respondent Annie Tang To Provide Proper Responses To Form Interrogatories And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: The Motion to Compel Respondent Annie Tang to Provide Proper Responses to Form Interrogatories and Request for Sanctions is continued on the Court's own motion to March 23, 2022, at 2:00 p.m.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JAMES WON LING BONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash And For Discovery Protective Order Regarding Document Subpoenas And Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: [1 of 2] This tentative is issued by Hon. Ross C. Moody. Before the court is Annie Yi Tang's Motion to Quash and for Discovery Protective Order Regarding Document Subpoenas and Monetary Sanctions. Mary Bong opposes the motion in its entirety and requests that sanctions be denied. Tang's motion to quash and for protective order is DENIED in part and GRANTED in part. It is well settled that the right of privacy is purely a personal one; it cannot be asserted by anyone other than the person whose privacy has been invaded. Lugosi v. Universal Pictures (1979) 25 Cal.3d 813, 820-821. The right does not survive but dies with the person. Hendrickson v. California Newspapers (1975) 48 Cal.App.3d 59, 62. James Wan Ling Bong's right of privacy died with him. While the motion argues that Annie Yi Tang and non-party Betty Bong have a right to privacy in the financial bank information subpoenaed, such a right has not been shown on this evidentiary record. The burden is on the moving party to establish "good cause" for whatever relief is requested. Tang bears the burden of production for this motion, and such burden must be met in the moving papers. Tang has failed to produce evidence that shows she has an interest in any of the bank accounts subpoenaed, much less a protected privacy interest. The moving party must establish a protectible privacy right before a protective order will be issued, and Tang has failed to do so. Assuming, arguendo, that Tang has an interest in the financial bank information subpoenaed, the burden is on Bong to show that the information is directly relevant to the issues in the case and there is no other source of less intrusive means to obtain the information. Bong's petition alleges that Tang or others improperly, and without decedent's authorization, transferred decedent's assets from his accounts. Decedent's bank records are directly relevant to the issue of whether or not transfers were made. Requesting the information directly from the banks appears to be the least intrusive and least costly way of obtaining this information. The Court finds that Bong has met her burden. Thus, the burden would then shift to Tang, "the party asserting a privacy interest[,] to establish [the] extent and the seriousness of the prospective invasion," and then the court must "weigh the countervailing interests [Tang] identifies." (Williams v. Sup. Ct. (Marshalls of CA, LLC) (2017) 3 Cal.5th 531, 557.) The Court must carefully balance the interests involved: i.e., the claimed right of privacy versus the public interest in obtaining just results in litigation. (Valley Bank of Nevada v. Sup. Ct. (Barkett) (1975) 15 c.3d 652, 657.) In considering the purpose of the information sought, the effect that disclosure will have on the parties and the trial, and the nature of Tang's objections, the Court finds that the balancing test weighs in Bong's favor.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JAMES WON LING BONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Respondent Annie Tang To Provide Proper Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: [1 of 3] This tentative is issued by Hon. Ross C. Moody. Before the court is Mary Bong's Motion to Compel Respondent Annie Tang to Provide Proper Responses to Requests for Production of Documents and Request for Sanctions. Tang's request for judicial notice is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. The request is granted as to the request to judicially notice Bong's First Amended Petition for Order Directing Conveyance or Transfer of Property to Probate Estate. Notice is limited to the relevant facts properly subject to notice given the materials presented and does not, for example, extend to the truth of assertions in pleadings or transcripts. (See Richtek USA, Inc. v. UPI Semiconductor Corp. (2015) 242 Cal.App.4th 651, 659-660.) The request is denied as to all other documents which include two civil judgments issued by the People's Court of Changing District in China and alleged translations of those judgments. To enable the court to take judicial notice of case files from another court, the party seeking such notice must either serve a subpoena duces tecum on the court clerk of the other court, or (preferably) obtain certified copies of the file in question and attach them to the moving papers. (Ross v. Creel Printing & Publishing Co., Inc. (2002) 100 Cal.App.4th 736, 743 (Nevada complaint not judicially noticeable because neither certified nor produced in response to a subpoena).) Bong's motion to compel further responses to Bong's Request for Production Set One is GRANTED. The responses to the request for production provided by Tang were not verified. Discovery responses must be verified or they are "tantamount to no responses at all." See Appleton v. Sup. Ct. (1988) 206 Cal.App.3d 632. Here the responses consisted of only objections. While a response with nothing other than objections does not need to be verified (CCP Section 2031.250(a), (c); see Blue Ridge Ins. Co. V. Sup. Ct. (Kippen) (1988) 202 Cal.App.3d 339), it is not clear whether the statute's requirements of good cause and meet-and-confer also apply to a response that is made up of only objections and therefore not verified. Even assuming, arguendo, that the unverified responses amount to responses that would require a motion to compel further responses rather than a motion to compel responses, the Court finds that Bong has provided sufficient good cause for the information and that Bong did materially meet-and-confer with Tang. As to the issue of relevance, the burden is on the moving party to show both: (1) relevance to the subject matter (e.g., how the information in the documents would tend to prove or disprove some issue in the case); and (2) specific facts justifying discovery (e.g., why such information is necessary for trial preparation or to prevent surprise at trial.) (Glenfed Develop. Corp. v. Sup. Ct. (National Union Fire Ins. Co. of Pittsburgh, Penn.) (1997) 53 Cal.App.4th 1113, 1117 (citations omitted); see also Kirkland v. Sup. Ct. (Guess? Inc.) (2002) 95 Cal.App.4th 92, 98.) Bong has shown that the information is relevant to the subject matter of the action and either admissible or "reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence."

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JAMES WON LING BONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Respondent Annie Tang To Provide Proper Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents And Request For Sanctions (Added For Posting Of Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: [3 of 3] Bong's request for sanctions is GRANTED. Bong is the prevailing party on this motion, and sanctions are mandatory. Furthermore, the Court finds that Tang has misused the discovery process by making unmeritorious objections to discovery without substantial justification in violation of CCP Section 2023.010(e). Tang shall pay to Bong $10,787.50 within ten days of service of notice of entry of this order. Mary Bong is ordered to lodge with the clerk in Department 204 a proposed order consistent with this tentative by the time set for this hearing Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the court day before the hearing. Remote appearance is strongly encouraged, in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JAMES WON LING BONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Respondent Annie Tang To Provide Proper Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents And Request For Sanctions (Added For Posting Of Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: [2 of 3] Accordingly, the burden shifts to the responding party to justify any objections made to document disclosure. (Kirkland at 98.) Generally, boilerplate objections that have the effect of obstructing discovery are improper and sanctionable. (See Korea Data Systems Co. Ltd. V. Sup. Ct. (Aamazing Technologies Corp.) (1997) 51 Cal.App.4th 1513, 1516.) In addition, objections based on "a fishing expedition" are generally not valid grounds for objecting to a request for production. (Greyhound Corp. v. Sup. Ct. (Clay) (1961) 56 Cal.2d 355, 384.) Furthermore, courts generally do not sustain ambiguous, confusing or overbroad discovery objections unless the question is totally unintelligible. The answering party owes a duty to respond in good faith as best he or she can. (See Deyo v. Kilbourne (1978) 84 Cal.App.3d 771, 783.) Tang's general objections are improper, and, overall, Tang failed to meet her burden. As to the issue of privacy, Tang has failed to provide evidence that she holds any interest in any of the bank accounts, much less met her burden to establish the extent and seriousness of the prospective invasion. If Tang had made such a showing, the court would then carefully balance the interests involved: i.e., the claimed right of privacy versus the public interest in obtaining just results in litigation. (Valley Bank of Nevada v. Sup. Ct. (Barkett) (1975) 15 c.3d 652, 657.) In considering the purpose of the information sought, the effect that disclosure will have on the parties and the trial, and the nature of Tang's objections, the balancing test would clearly weigh in Bong's favor. As to the issue of privilege, the burden is on Tang to establish whatever preliminary facts are essential to her claims of privilege. Tang has failed to state a specific privilege that bars discovery or "provide sufficient factual information for other parties to evaluate the merits of that claim, including, if necessary a privilege log." (CCP Section 2031.240(c)(1) and (2).) The purpose of a privilege log is to make a record of the documents withheld and the privilege claim asserted as to each. (Wellpoint Health Networks, Inc. v. Sup. Ct. (McCombs) (1997) 59 Cal.App.4th 110, 130.) Again, Tang has not met her burden. For the foregoing reasons, Bong's Motion to Compel Respondent Annie Tang to Provide Proper Responses to Requests for Production of Documents is GRANTED. Because the only relevant timeframe for allegedly improper transfers expired upon the decedent's death, the instant discovery is limited as to Requests Number 11 through 29 to the period prior to June 3, 2019, the death of James Won Ling Bong. Tang is ordered to respond to the requests for production of documents within twenty days of service of notice of entry of this order.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JAMES WON LING BONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash And For Discovery Protective Order Regarding Document Subpoenas And Monetary Sanctions (Added For Posting Of Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: [2 of 2] Because the only relevant timeframe for allegedly improper transfers expired upon the decedent's death, the motion to quash is GRANTED as to records and information after June 3, 2019, the date of death of James Won Ling Bong. The motion to quash is DENIED as to all records and information prior to June 3, 2019. Tang's request for sanctions is DENIED. Tang is not the prevailing party on this motion. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROSALIE LUM WONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: continued to 3/1/22. Petitioner must mail notice again on the proper form - DE-120, using the new hearing date. POS must be filed by noon on 2/22/22. in addition, Petitioner must provide a revised order that has a second page for attachment 5a. please note, the order provided says 53 pages attached, but none were attached, and it would not be appropriate to attach 53 pages to this order, just one page with the accounts identified. deadline for providing that order to the examiner in room 202 is noon on 2/22/22. Petitioner notified of the issues by email on 2/7/22 at 10 a.m.
